description:
You are a compliance officer performing a routine integrity audit on a production-like workstation (no root privileges).  
A critical document is stored at

    /home/user/compliance_docs/passwd_policy_v1.2.txt

Your job is to verify its SHA-256 checksum and record the result.

Required result:
1. Produce a *single* terminal command that
   • calculates the SHA-256 digest of the file above, **and**  
   • writes the digest only (exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters, followed by a single newline, nothing else) into  

        /home/user/audit_log/passwd_policy.sha256

   The audit_log directory may not exist yet – create it within the same command if necessary (you may combine operators, pipes, redirection, etc., but it must still be one compound command).
2. After the command finishes, the file /home/user/audit_log/passwd_policy.sha256 must exist and contain **only** the digest line described above.
3. Nothing else should be written to the terminal apart from what the command naturally emits (no extra echo statements).

The automated test will:
• read /home/user/audit_log/passwd_policy.sha256  
• verify that it consists of exactly 64 lowercase hex characters plus a trailing newline, and  
• independently compute the SHA-256 digest of /home/user/compliance_docs/passwd_policy_v1.2.txt, ensuring the two values match.

You do NOT need to display or hard-code the expected hash; just compute it.  
Use standard GNU/Linux CLI utilities available in a minimal distribution (e.g. sha256sum, mkdir, tee, cut, awk, etc.).  
Accomplish everything with a single shell command.
truth:
Initial state before agent starts:

1. The file /home/user/compliance_docs/passwd_policy_v1.2.txt exists, is 0 bytes long (completely empty), and is world-readable (0644).

2. No /home/user/audit_log directory is present.

Ground-truth checksum (empty file SHA-256):

    e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

Verification criteria after agent finishes:

A. The file /home/user/audit_log/passwd_policy.sha256 exists, is world-readable (0644 or stricter), and has the exact single line:

    e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\n

(no leading/trailing spaces).

B. No additional files are created in /home/user/audit_log.

C. The command executed must be a single shell command (possibly using command chaining, subshells, or pipes, but not multiple separate commands entered interactively).

The automated test will read the file, compare the content to the string above, and also recompute the digest of /home/user/compliance_docs/passwd_policy_v1.2.txt to ensure consistency.
